div.p: Why A20 exists: The 8086 (1978) had only 20 address lines. Addresses above 1MB would wrap around to 0. Some early software relied on this! When the 286 added more address lines, IBM added a gate to disable A20 for compatibility. Modern CPUs don't need it, but the gate remains for legacy support.

g.text: test al, 2 ; Check if already on
